text.title: Agile BA Frameworks Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Assign specific BA deliverables to each Scrum phase. Treating BA work as a single undifferentiated 'refinement' activity is rejected.
rule.rationale: Unassigned BA activities are skipped or done ad-hoc, causing AC defects to surface only at Sprint Review.

rule.statement: Map BA role to SAFe level BEFORE PI Planning. Default 'team-level only' rejected when initiative requires Program or Portfolio BA work.
rule.rationale: SAFe lacks an explicit BA role; without explicit mapping, BA defaults to team-level tasks even when initiative needs Program/Portfolio scope.

rule.statement: Select agile BA technique by purpose, not familiarity. Each technique has a distinct trigger (user stories vs example mapping vs story mapping vs impact mapping).
rule.rationale: Default-to-user-stories leaves discovery gaps that surface as missed requirements after Sprint Review.

rule.statement: Score each of the 7 IIBA Agile-Extension principles with a quoted evidence line. Reject any score row lacking a citation.
rule.rationale: Unevidenced scores reduce to opinion; only cited artefacts survive CCBA/CBAP audit and provide actionable gap data.

rule.statement: Choose ONE canonical framework as the anchor and label techniques borrowed from others explicitly. Mixed IIBA AE + DA + SAFe vocabulary without declared anchor is rejected.
rule.rationale: Mixed vocabulary creates false precision and forces future BAs to relitigate framework choices every sprint.

rule.statement: Select a single DA primary lifecycle plus at most one alternative. 'All lifecycles apply' is rejected.
rule.rationale: DA documents 6 lifecycles; an agent given all options picks all, making the choice useless for team planning.

rule.statement: Run framework fit as a one-shot decision on initiative setup or major team/scope change — NOT a periodic cron.
rule.rationale: Framework choices are not daily tasks; scheduled re-runs produce stale recommendations that erode trust.

rule.statement: Pin framework versions in the constitution; refresh fit-report on major team or scope change (M&A, regulatory shift, team-size doubling).
rule.rationale: Without version pins, agent output drifts as upstream content updates; without refresh triggers, recommendations rot.

rule.statement: Skip for single-team Scrum with no scaling concern and no regulatory bar OR greenfield product discovery (use continuous-discovery) OR non-software domain (vocabulary misapplies).
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead exceeds value on simple Scrum; cross-domain misapplication creates false precision.
